best.explanation: Belgas looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 68 kJ, 8 s period, workable consistency 54/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 58 kJ, 8 s period, lully 25/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 58 kJ, 8 s period, workable consistency 51/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind.  Watch: Cerro may feel too soft without more push; gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk.

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0-0.3 m, 12 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and good wind at 13 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Supertubos looks below your bodyboard fun size.
